name: Mcnb Bank And Trust Co.
description_short: MCNB Bank and Trust Co. is a community bank headquartered in Welch, WV, offering traditional banking services including checking, savings, and trust services with FDIC insurance protection.
description_long:
MCNB Bank and Trust Co. is a regional bank based in Welch, West Virginia that provides full-service banking and financial services to individuals and businesses. The bank has established itself as a community financial institution committed to serving local customers with traditional banking products and personalized service. The company maintains an active online presence and offers digital banking capabilities to complement its in-person services.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services including checking accounts, savings accounts, certificate of deposit (CD) products, and trust services. Customers can access their accounts through multiple channels: online banking via iBank, business banking through iBank Business, a mobile app, phone banking at 800-532-9562, and in-person branch visits. The bank also provides financial services through its Payday Alternative Loan (PAL) program, which offers small loans to members. All deposits are FDIC-insured, providing customers with federal protection backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

MCNB distinguishes itself through its commitment to accessibility and inclusive digital design. The bank has implemented comprehensive accessibility features including text magnification up to 3x, color adjustment tools, dyslexic-friendly fonts, keyboard navigation, screen reader compatibility, and text-to-speech functionality. The website explicitly strives to conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards and ADA compliance requirements. This focus on accessibility across all user abilities reflects a broader commitment to serving diverse community members and providing equitable access to financial services.

As a regional community bank, MCNB operates with the limitations typical of smaller institutions. The website content provided focuses heavily on accessibility features and login portals rather than detailed product information, rates, or fee structures. Potential customers should contact the bank directly at 800-532-9562 or visit a local branch for specific information about account features, interest rates, minimum balances, and service fees. The bank's strength lies in personalized community banking rather than the extensive product lineup or competitive rates that larger national banks may offer. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Multiple access channels: online banking (iBank), mobile app, phone (800-532-9562), and in-person branches
Comprehensive accessibility features including text magnification, dyslexic fonts, screen reader compatibility, and WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance efforts
Offers Payday Alternative Loans (PALs) as a lower-cost borrowing option
Full-service trust services available for estate planning and wealth management
Community-focused institution with local presence in West Virginia
Secure email and live chat support available for customer assistance
cons: Limited geographic footprint as a regional bank restricts convenience for customers outside West Virginia service area
Website content lacks transparency on specific account features, interest rates, minimum balances, and fee structures
No information provided about competitive APY rates compared to national banks or online banks
Limited details on small business lending products despite offering business banking services
Accessibility statement includes legal disclaimer that the bank cannot guarantee full accessibility to all users
best_for: West Virginia residents seeking community banking with personalized service and local branch access
Customers with accessibility needs who require screen readers, text magnification, dyslexic fonts, or other assistive technologies
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured deposit products and basic banking services from an established regional institution
Small business owners in the MCNB service area needing basic business checking and banking services
diagnosis: MCNB Bank and Trust Co. is best for West Virginia residents and small business owners seeking community banking with personalized service, FDIC insurance protection, and strong accessibility features. The main caveat is that as a regional bank, it has limited geographic reach and the website provides minimal information about specific products, rates, and fees—prospective customers must contact the bank directly for detailed account information and comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
